wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the NutriTrack project overview section. Explain its purpose as a Thai-language nutrition and calorie tracking web application designed for weight loss management. Document the key features including interactive calorie ring visualization, macronutrient monitoring, quick food addition system, manual food entry, organized food diary, and data persistence using localStorage. Describe the target audience of users seeking to track their daily food intake and maintain dietary goals. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'calorie ring', 'macros', 'preset foods', and 'food diary'. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like logging meals, tracking progress, and managing nutritional goals.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard & Progress Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the dashboard and progress tracking features of NutriTrack. Document the interactive SVG-based calorie ring visualization that shows real-time progress toward daily calorie goals, including the gradient stroke animation and color changes when exceeding targets. Explain the macronutrient tracking system with animated progress bars for protein, carbohydrates, and fats, showing current intake versus goals with percentage indicators. Detail how the dashboard automatically calculates totals from all logged foods and updates visual elements in real-time. Include technical implementation details of the SVG circle calculations, CSS custom properties for dynamic progress, and the renderAll() function that orchestrates all dashboard updates. Document the responsive design patterns and accessibility considerations for the progress indicators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for NutriTrack's single-file SPA architecture. Describe how all HTML, CSS, and JavaScript are contained within one index.html file following a monolithic approach suitable for simple applications. Document the module pattern implementation with encapsulated functionality within functions, the observer pattern for real-time UI updates through renderAll(), and the event-driven architecture using DOM event listeners. Explain the component relationships between state management (GOALS, MEALS, PRESETS, foodLog), UI rendering engine, data management layer, and event handlers. Include the application initialization flow from init() function, dependency analysis between components, and extension points for customization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for NutriTrack. Explain how to run the application by simply opening the index.html file in any modern web browser. Provide step-by-step instructions for first-time users including setting up initial goals, adding their first food entries using both preset options and manual form input, and understanding the dashboard interface. Document the basic workflow: selecting meal categories, choosing from preset Thai foods or entering custom items, viewing real-time progress updates, and managing the food diary. Include guidance on browser compatibility requirements and mobile device usage. Address initial setup considerations and provide troubleshooting tips for common issues like localStorage access permissions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Food Logging System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the food logging system in NutriTrack. Document both the quick preset addition feature with 8 common Thai foods (chicken breast, chicken rice, boiled eggs, whey protein, sticky rice with grilled pork, papaya salad, tom yum noodles, bananas) and the manual food entry form with validation. Explain the preset button system that dynamically generates clickable food items with nutritional information display. Detail the manual form implementation including input validation, meal category selection, and real-time data processing. Document the addFood() function that creates standardized food item objects with unique IDs, timestamps, and nutritional data. Include error handling, user feedback through toast notifications, and the seamless integration between preset and manual logging methods. Address form reset functionality and data consistency across both logging approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for NutriTrack's state management system. Thoroughly explain the global variable approach using GOALS object for nutritional targets (calories: 1800, protein: 120g, carbs: 150g, fats: 50g), MEALS configuration for Thai meal categories, PRESETS array containing 8 common Thai foods with nutritional information, and foodLog array as the main data store. Document the state lifecycle from initialization through localStorage loading, state mutations via addFood() and deleteFood() functions, and state persistence using save(). Include concrete examples of state structure, data validation rules, and the relationship between state changes and UI re-rendering through the observer pattern.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the core features section of NutriTrack. Document the main functional areas including the interactive dashboard with SVG-based calorie ring visualization, real-time macronutrient tracking with progress bars, quick preset food addition system with 8 common Thai foods, manual food entry form with validation, organized food diary with meal categorization, and automatic data persistence. Explain how each feature works together to provide a seamless nutrition tracking experience. Include detailed descriptions of user interactions, visual feedback mechanisms, and data flow between components. Document the relationship between different UI elements and how changes in one area automatically update others through the centralized state management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Food Diary Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the food diary management system in NutriTrack. Detail the organized display of logged meals categorized into four sections: breakfast (มื้อเช้า), lunch (มื้อกลางวัน), dinner (มื้อเย็น), and snacks (ของว่าง). Explain the meal-specific card layout with gradient headers, individual food item displays showing nutritional breakdown (calories, protein, carbs, fats), and timestamp information. Document the automatic meal total calculations that update in real-time as foods are added or removed. Detail the delete functionality for individual food items with hover-reveal delete buttons and confirmation feedback. Explain the empty state handling with placeholder messages and the smooth animations for adding/removing items. Include the data filtering logic that organizes foodLog entries by meal category and the responsive grid layout that adapts to different screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Rendering System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for NutriTrack's UI rendering system. Detail the central renderAll() function that orchestrates all dashboard updates including calorie ring visualization, macronutrient progress bars, and food diary display. Document the SVG-based calorie ring implementation using CSS custom properties (--p) and stroke-dashoffset animations, the dynamic macro bar width calculations based on goal percentages, and the meal-specific list rendering with individual item deletion capabilities. Explain the real-time update mechanism triggered by state changes, the toast notification system for user feedback, and the responsive layout handling using Tailwind CSS utility classes. Include performance considerations for DOM manipulation and animation optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technical Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for NutriTrack's technical implementation. Describe the single-file SPA architecture where all HTML, CSS, and JavaScript are contained within one index.html file. Document the state management approach using global variables (GOALS, MEALS, PRESETS, foodLog), the event-driven rendering system with renderAll() function, and the observer pattern implementation for real-time UI updates. Explain the data flow from user input through form handlers to state updates and DOM manipulation. Document the localStorage integration for data persistence, the SVG animation system for the calorie ring, and the responsive design patterns using Tailwind CSS utility classes. Include component relationships, dependency analysis, and extension points for customization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Persistence & State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the data persistence and state management system in NutriTrack. Document the localStorage implementation for maintaining user data across browser sessions, including the save() function that serializes the foodLog array and the initialization code that loads existing data on application startup. Explain the centralized state management approach using global variables (GOALS, MEALS, PRESETS, foodLog) and how changes trigger automatic UI updates through the renderAll() observer pattern. Detail the data model structure for food items including unique ID generation, timestamp creation, and nutritional value storage. Document the resetAll() function for clearing all data with user confirmation dialogs. Include error handling for localStorage operations, data validation during form submissions, and the synchronization between state changes and DOM updates. Address performance considerations for frequent localStorage operations and potential data migration strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Persistence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for NutriTrack's data persistence layer using browser localStorage API. Document the save() function that serializes the foodLog array to JSON format and stores it under the 'nutritrack_log' key, the initialization process that loads existing data from localStorage during app startup, and the data recovery mechanism when users return to the application. Explain the data structure format for food items including unique IDs generated with Date.now() + Math.random(), timestamp formatting using Thai locale, and the complete reset functionality through resetAll() with confirmation dialogs. Include error handling strategies, data validation before storage, and best practices for localStorage usage in web applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration and customization documentation for NutriTrack. Document how to modify nutritional goals by editing the GOALS object (calories: 1800, protein: 120g, carbs: 150g, fats: 50g). Explain how to add new preset foods to the PRESETS array with proper nutritional information structure. Document theme customization options including brand color modifications in the Tailwind CSS configuration, font adjustments, and styling overrides. Provide guidance on extending meal categories, modifying the food item structure, and implementing additional features. Include examples of common customizations and best practices for maintaining code organization when making modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling & Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for NutriTrack's styling and design system built with Tailwind CSS and custom CSS. Document the brand color palette configuration extending Tailwind's theme with green gradient colors (brand-50 to brand-900), the Noto Sans Thai font integration for Thai language support, and custom CSS animations including fadeIn, slideIn, and smooth transitions for interactive elements. Explain the responsive design patterns using Tailwind's grid system and utility classes, the CSS custom properties approach for dynamic styling (like --p for calorie ring progress), and the hover/active states for buttons and cards. Include the visual hierarchy principles, spacing conventions, and accessibility considerations built into the design system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for NutriTrack. Address common issues such as localStorage not working due to browser security settings, data persistence problems across different browsers, mobile device compatibility issues, and performance considerations for large datasets. Document known limitations of the single-file architecture and workarounds for specific use cases. Include frequently asked questions about data backup, export functionality, goal setting recommendations, and nutritional accuracy. Provide debugging tips using browser developer tools, error handling strategies, and steps for reporting issues. Address browser-specific behaviors and provide solutions for cross-platform compatibility concerns.

</think>

Based on my analysis of the NutriTrack project, I can now generate a comprehensive overview. This is a single-page web application for nutrition tracking with Thai language support.

<blog>

# NutriTrack - Nutrition Tracking Application

## Project Introduction

**NutriTrack** is a comprehensive nutrition and calorie tracking web application designed specifically for weight loss management. The application provides users with an intuitive interface to track their daily food intake, monitor macronutrient consumption, and maintain dietary goals through a visually appealing dashboard.

### Purpose Statement
The primary purpose of NutriTrack is to help users effectively manage their nutrition and achieve weight loss goals by providing real-time tracking of calories and macronutrients (protein, carbohydrates, and fats) with a user-friendly Thai-language interface.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Calorie Tracking**: Monitor daily caloric intake against personalized goals
- **Macronutrient Management**: Track protein, carbohydrate, and fat consumption
- **Meal Organization**: Categorize food intake into breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks
- **Quick Food Logging**: Provide preset food options for rapid entry
- **Visual Progress Monitoring**: Display progress through interactive charts and indicators
- **Data Persistence**: Maintain user data across sessions using local storage

### Target Audience
- Thai-speaking individuals seeking weight loss solutions
- Health-conscious users monitoring their nutritional intake
- Fitness enthusiasts tracking macronutrient ratios
- Users preferring mobile-responsive web applications over native apps

## Technical Architecture

The NutriTrack application follows a **Single Page Application (SPA)** architecture built entirely with vanilla JavaScript, HTML5, and CSS3. The application leverages modern web technologies while maintaining simplicity and performance.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[HTML Structure] --> B[Tailwind CSS Styling]
    A --> C[JavaScript Logic]
    C --> D[Local Storage API]
    C --> E[DOM Manipulation]
    C --> F[Event Handling]
    B --> G[Responsive Design]
    D --> H[Data Persistence]
    E --> I[Real-time Updates]
    F --> J[User Interactions]
    
    subgraph "Core Components"
        K[Dashboard UI]
        L[Food Entry Form]
        M[Preset Foods]
        N[Progress Visualization]
    end
    
    A --> K
    A --> L
    A --> M
    A --> N
```

### Component Breakdown

The application is structured around several key components:

1. **Dashboard Component**: Central hub displaying calorie ring, macro bars, and progress indicators
2. **Food Entry System**: Form-based input for manual food logging
3. **Preset Foods Module**: Quick-add functionality with predefined nutritional values
4. **Food Diary**: Organized display of logged meals by category
5. **Data Management Layer**: Local storage integration for persistence

### Design Patterns

The application implements several design patterns:

- **Module Pattern**: Encapsulating related functionality within functions
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time UI updates when data changes
- **Factory Pattern**: Creating food items with consistent structure
- **State Management**: Centralized state handling through global variables

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[Form Handler]
    B --> C[Data Validation]
    C --> D[Food Item Creation]
    D --> E[Local Storage Update]
    E --> F[UI Re-rendering]
    F --> G[Progress Calculation]
    G --> H[Visual Feedback]
    
    I[Preset Selection] --> J[Quick Add Function]
    J --> D
    
    K[Delete Action] --> L[Data Removal]
    L --> E
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Primary File**: `index.html` - Complete single-file application containing HTML structure, CSS styling, and JavaScript logic

### Core Modules

#### State Management
The application maintains application state through global variables:
- `GOALS`: User's nutritional targets (calories, protein, carbs, fats)
- `MEALS`: Meal category definitions
- `PRESETS`: Predefined food items with nutritional information
- `foodLog`: Array storing all logged food entries

#### Data Persistence Layer
- **Storage Mechanism**: Browser's localStorage API
- **Data Format**: JSON serialization for complex objects
- **Auto-save**: Automatic data persistence on every modification

#### UI Rendering Engine
- **Dynamic DOM Manipulation**: Real-time updates without page reloads
- **CSS Animations**: Smooth transitions and visual feedback
- **Responsive Layout**: Mobile-first design using Tailwind CSS grid system

### Configuration Approach
- **Inline Configuration**: All settings defined within the HTML file
- **Tailwind CSS Customization**: Theme configuration for brand colors
- **Goal Settings**: Default nutritional targets hardcoded in JavaScript

### External Dependencies
- **Tailwind CSS**: Utility-first CSS framework loaded via CDN
- **Google Fonts**: Thai font support (Noto Sans Thai)
- **SVG Graphics**: Custom calorie ring visualization

### Integration Points
- **Browser APIs**: localStorage, Date object, Math utilities
- **CSS Custom Properties**: Dynamic styling based on data
- **Event Listeners**: Form submission, button clicks, user interactions

### Component Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Main App] --> B[Dashboard]
    A --> C[Food Entry]
    A --> D[Preset Foods]
    A --> E[Food Diary]
    
    B --> F[Calorie Ring]
    B --> G[Macro Bars]
    B --> H[Progress Stats]
    
    C --> I[Validation]
    C --> J[Data Processing]
    
    D --> K[Quick Add]
    
    E --> L[Meal Categories]
    E --> M[Item Management]
    
    F --> N[SVG Animation]
    G --> O[CSS Transitions]
    
    J --> P[localStorage]
    K --> P
    M --> P
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### 1. Calorie Tracking Dashboard
- **Interactive Calorie Ring**: SVG-based circular progress indicator showing daily calorie consumption
- **Real-time Calculations**: Instant updates as users add or remove food items
- **Goal Comparison**: Visual indication of remaining calories and over-consumption warnings

#### 2. Macronutrient Monitoring
- **Protein Tracking**: Blue-themed progress bar with gram-based measurements
- **Carbohydrate Monitoring**: Amber-colored progress indicator for carb intake
- **Fat Consumption**: Pink-themed tracker for dietary fat monitoring
- **Percentage Badges**: Color-coded status indicators for each macronutrient

#### 3. Quick Food Addition System
- **Preset Foods Database**: 8 common Thai foods with accurate nutritional information
- **Meal Category Selection**: Dropdown menu for organizing food entries
- **One-click Logging**: Rapid food addition without form filling

#### 4. Comprehensive Food Diary
- **Meal Organization**: Separate sections for breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks
- **Detailed Food Entries**: Each item displays name, calories, macros, and timestamp
- **Individual Deletion**: Hover-reveal delete buttons for precise meal management
- **Meal Subtotals**: Running totals for each meal category

#### 5. Data Management
- **Persistent Storage**: Automatic saving to browser localStorage
- **Session Recovery**: Data restoration on page reload
- **Reset Functionality**: Complete data clearing with confirmation dialog

### Implementation Highlights

#### Advanced UI Features
- **Gradient Backgrounds**: Modern gradient color schemes throughout the interface
- **Glass Morphism Effects**: Semi-transparent backgrounds with backdrop blur
- **Micro-interactions**: Hover effects, scale animations, and smooth transitions
- **Toast Notifications**: Non-intrusive feedback messages for user actions

#### Responsive Design
- **Mobile-First Approach**: Optimized layout for various screen sizes
- **Touch-Friendly Interface**: Large tap targets and swipe-friendly elements
- **Adaptive Grid System**: Flexible layouts that adjust to container width

#### Performance Optimizations
- **Minimal DOM Manipulation**: Efficient rendering with selective updates
- **CSS Hardware Acceleration**: GPU-accelerated animations for smooth performance
- **LocalStorage Efficiency**: Batched data operations to minimize storage writes

### Feature Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialize
    Initialize --> LoadData: Read localStorage
    LoadData --> RenderDashboard: Display initial state
    
    RenderDashboard --> AddFood: User submits form
    RenderDashboard --> AddPreset: User selects preset
    RenderDashboard --> DeleteItem: User removes food
    
    AddFood --> ValidateInput: Check required fields
    ValidateInput --> SaveData: Valid input
    ValidateInput --> ShowError: Invalid input
    
    AddPreset --> CreateFoodItem: Generate from preset
    CreateFoodItem --> SaveData
    
    DeleteItem --> RemoveFromLog: Filter out deleted item
    RemoveFromLog --> SaveData
    
    SaveData --> UpdateCalculations: Recalculate totals
    UpdateCalculations --> RefreshUI: Re-render components
    RefreshUI --> RenderDashboard
    
    ShowError --> RenderDashboard: After error display
```

### Data Flow Architecture

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant UI as Interface
    participant S as State Manager
    participant LS as Local Storage
    participant R as Renderer
    
    U->>+UI: Submit food entry
    UI->>+S: Process new food item
    S->>+LS: Save to storage
    LS-->>-S: Confirm save
    S->>+R: Trigger re-render
    R->>+UI: Update display
    UI-->>-U: Show success feedback
    
    Note over S,R: Real-time calculations and updates
```

</blog>
